title: Snapp
summary: Frontend engineer on Snapp's ride products — from the cab team to intercity rides, shipping React/Next.js experiences for millions of users.

narrative.problem: A consumer super-app serving millions needed fast, reliable frontends across devices and network conditions.
narrative.challenge: Ship ride-booking flows at scale without sacrificing performance, accessibility, or maintainability across teams.
narrative.process: Started on the cab team, then moved into intercity rides — building on a shared UI kit, Redux Toolkit, SWR, and mobile-first patterns.
narrative.solution: Production PWAs and responsive web apps for urban and intercity ride experiences, with strong TypeScript boundaries.
narrative.outcome: Millions of users rely on the experience daily — from city cabs to intercity trips, performance and reliability stay non-negotiable.

title: Jaan
summary: Built the admin panel for Jaan — a Persian mindfulness app with guided courses, breathing exercises, and self-care tracking for 100k+ Play Store users.

narrative.problem: Jaan's content and operations team needed a reliable way to manage courses, users, and wellness content at scale.
narrative.challenge: Build an admin panel that fits RTL workflows, stays maintainable, and ships alongside a live consumer app.
narrative.process: React components, SCSS design system, Jest coverage, and iterative releases with product and content teams.
narrative.solution: A production admin panel for managing Jaan's guided courses, breathing content, and self-care features.
narrative.outcome: 100k+ downloads on Google Play — the admin tooling I built helped the team ship and iterate on a live wellness product.

title: Metro Man
summary: Solo-built offline iOS metro navigator for Tehran — schematic maps, shortest-path routing, live journey mode, and gamification across 150 stations.

narrative.problem: Tehran metro riders need fast offline route planning and clear in-station guidance — often with no reliable network underground.
narrative.challenge: Design and ship the entire app solo: bundled station data, routing, schematic maps, RTL UI, GPS journey tracking, and gamification.
narrative.process: Layered architecture with protocol-based DI, Dijkstra routing over 150 stations, Canvas map rendering, and XCTest coverage across domain and UI layers.
narrative.solution: A fully offline native iOS app with step-by-step journey mode, achievement tracking, and Persian-first design.
narrative.outcome: Published on Sibapp — an end-to-end product from data modeling to App Store assets, built and maintained by me.

title: Rojino
summary: Solo-built iOS lipstick try-on app — live camera preview, Vision lip tracking, shade comparison, and RTL product flows in Persian.

narrative.problem: Beauty shoppers struggle to compare lipstick shades and see how a color looks on their own lips before buying.
narrative.challenge: Build the full app solo — real-time lip contour tracking without a paid AR SDK, plus comparison flows and RTL product UI.
narrative.process: Vision face landmarks for lip detection, blended CAShapeLayer rendering, shade picker with finish toggles, and Persian localisation throughout.
narrative.solution: A camera-first iOS app where users try shades live, compare favorites side-by-side, and browse product details in fa-IR.
narrative.outcome: Published on Sibapp — a complete AR beauty experience designed, implemented, and shipped by me.

title: Zerang
summary: A programming language and Rust interpreter built from scratch — lexer, parser, evaluator, and a growing standard library.

narrative.problem: I wanted to understand languages deeply — not just use them, but design one and make it run.
narrative.challenge: Build a coherent grammar, a safe runtime, and useful builtins without leaning on an existing interpreter.
narrative.process: Hand-written lexer and parser in Rust, AST evaluation, error reporting, and iterative language design.
narrative.solution: Seyed Zerang — a small but real language with variables, control flow, functions, and a growing stdlib.
narrative.outcome: A working interpreter on GitHub — 34 commits of hands-on compiler construction and language design.

title: ILIA Corporation
summary: First industry internship at a management consulting firm — learning how teams plan, communicate, and deliver.

narrative.problem: I needed real workplace experience alongside university.
narrative.challenge: Translate classroom knowledge into professional collaboration inside a consulting environment.
narrative.process: Task management, written communication, and team workflows across client-facing projects.
narrative.solution: Showed up, learned, and contributed where I could — observing how strategy work gets done at scale.
narrative.outcome: The first step from student to engineer — and my introduction to how organizations actually ship.
